keyPoints: Adaptive display intensity aligned with task demand, user expertise, and curiosity.
Personalize DNL cues via expertise profiles and opt-in controls with adaptive tuning.
Align labeling with task type: strong cues for high-control tasks; milder cues for routine tasks.
Frame cues to support mastery and autonomy while avoiding undermining intrinsic motivation through extrinsic rewards.
Incorporate fatigue safeguards: breaks, reflective prompts, throttling, and transparent opt-out options.
Plan controlled validation across display intensity, task type, and expertise; measure motivation, fatigue, intrinsic interest, and long-term engagement.
Acknowledge boundary conditions: novelty dynamics, habituation, and the need for varied, context-relevant cues.
